steps.title: Spot the contradiction between explanations
steps.content: The passage first attributes the barnacles' movement to passive displacement by water currents. The research finding—that the barnacles “moved...against the prevailing water flow”—contradicts this. The transition must signal that the new finding undermines the previous explanation.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Undermining this explanation” correctly opposes the passive-displacement hypothesis. The other three either affirm the hypothesis rather than undermine it, confuse what the finding concludes, or phrase the relationship awkwardly. That matches Choice B — Undermining this explanation,.
